channel.title: Deimar Gutiérrez — Blog
channel.description: Fresh perspectives on finance, founders, and operational reality.

item.title: 3D Printing Brings New Business Opportunities
item.description: 3D printed High-Heels How do you make a single, custom part? For decades, you didn't. You ordered a batch, or you machined it. But that's changing.

item.title: 4 Words to Make You Persuasive
item.description: You’re trying to get a client to sign, a team to adopt a new process, or a supplier to cut a deal. What if four simple words could shift the conversation? Dr.

item.title: 5 Expenses Your Startup Can't Afford to Overlook
item.description: You've got a business idea, a team, maybe even a first customer. You've built a budget, but what did you miss? Most founders, sitting at their kitchen table…

item.title: 6 Rules to Stop Competing On Price
item.description: You’ve seen it happen. A competitor slashes prices, or maybe you've considered it yourself, hoping to grab more market share. It feels like a quick win. It’s not. It’s a trap.

item.title: 86% Fail to Meet Promised Projections: How Start-Ups Lose Credibility and Investors
item.description: The Cost of Inflated Pitches You're in the room. The pitch deck slides across the conference table. Your numbers look good, maybe *too* good.

item.title: The 9 To-Do's of Successful Entrepreneurs
item.description: 1. Know your purpose Before you launch a new business, understand the market. What are you building? Why does it matter? Where does this business lead? Your answers shape how you run the company.

item.title: Accountability in Business: Focus on Growth, Not Blame
item.description: The email lands at 6 PM. Sarah from your sales team just lost a $50,000 project. They missed a key deadline. You feel the familiar knot in your stomach: who dropped the ball?

item.title: The acquisition offer you didn't take seriously enough
item.description: It came in at $40M when the company was worth $30. He laughed. Eighteen months later, the company was worth $12 and the buyer had moved on.

item.title: The advisor you only call after you've already decided
item.description: He's been on your cap table for three years. You've called him twice. Both times, you wanted permission, not advice.

item.title: AI in Marketing: Opportunities and Challenges
item.description: Your marketing budget feels tight, but the competition's ads seem to hit harder, more often. You wonder if they've found a secret lever. They might have.

item.title: Aim for the Right Goals to Reach Business Success
item.description: Climb the Right Mountain: Achieving Business Success You're staring at the P&L, a stack of invoices on your desk. Another month, another 15% revenue bump. Good, right?

item.description: Marketing today goes beyond the marketing department. Every employee fortifies the company's ability to spread its message and captivate target customers. Companies spent a lot of time, money, and

item.title: The all-hands that became a status report
item.description: Sixty people sat through forty-five minutes of updates they could have read in a doc. Nobody asked a question. Nobody was supposed to.

item.title: Are You S.M.A.R.T When Setting Goals?
item.description: To be successful is not necessary to work 25 hours a day, 8 days a week, one must be SMART. The fact is that when you fail to effectively define your goals , your work and effort lose their impact.

item.title: Your biggest customer is teaching you the wrong product
item.description: The customer who pays the most has leverage to shape your roadmap. Building what they ask doesn't win the next 200 buyers.

item.title: The board update that buried the bad news
item.description: Page nine, paragraph three, fourth sentence in. The number that mattered most was hidden where nobody would ask about it.

item.description: Same price per share. New terms in the legal language. The founder discovered the down round in a footnote eight months later.

item.title: Building Loyalty From The First Interview
item.description: A successful employer-employee relationship requires mutual investment to create common value. From the first interview, both must identify if they share common goals that they can achieve together.

item.description: Their logo was on the homepage. Their quote was in the deck. They had canceled six weeks before the marketing team noticed.

item.description: Invoices went out on the first. Money landed somewhere between day forty and day ninety. Nobody could explain the difference because nobody was watching it.

item.title: The Proposal Didn't Lose the Deal
item.description: Sales teams review lost deals by inspecting the proposal. That's the wrong artifact. The deal died earlier, and the proposal just recorded the time of death.

item.title: Stop Calling It Delegation
item.description: Trust is not the absence of the manager. It is a specific structure most managers skip because they mistake it for micromanagement.
